Thanks!

title: eCommerce / Virtual Assistant/Shopify Expert
description:
Hi — I'm Ricco, founder of ThrivingMoms (
).



ThrivingMoms is a Shopify print-on-demand brand selling apparel, mugs, and lifestyle products for moms. We're a small, focused brand with a clear identity — not a generic POD store. Every product carries a specific phrase and emotional tone that speaks directly to our customer.



I'm looking for a reliable VA to help run the day-to-day operations of the store so I can focus on strategy, product development, and marketing.



This is a long-term part-time role. If you do great work and the brand grows, your hours grow with it.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO EACH WEEK:



- Check Shopify orders daily — flag any issues or fulfillment delays

- Monitor and reply to customer
s within 4 hours (templates provided)

- Upload new product listings to Shopify using our exact naming and SEO format

- Coordinate with Printify/Printful to ensure products are synced correctly

- Manage Etsy listings — add new digital products, update tags and descriptions

- Schedule 3 social media posts per week using our approved content calendar

- Send a brief weekly performance summary every Friday (orders, revenue, issues)



---



WHAT I NEED FROM YOU:



- Direct experience with Shopify Admin (not just "familiar with" — you need to have done it)

- Experience with Printify OR Printful — product syncing, variants, fulfillment monitoring

- Experience managing an Etsy seller account

- Strong written English — you will be writing customer replies in our brand voice

- Organized and self-directed — I don't want to chase you for updates

- Available during US morning or afternoon hours (overlap with CST preferred)

- Reliable internet connection and consistent availability



---



BONUS (not required, but valued):



- Pinterest scheduling experience

- Klaviyo or
 marketing familiarity

- Experience with a lifestyle, apparel, or POD brand specifically



---



WHAT THIS IS NOT:



- This is not a general VA role. If you haven't worked with Shopify and POD before, please don't apply.

- This is not a social media manager role. Content creation is not the primary job.

- This is not a short-term project. I'm looking for someone who wants to grow with the brand.



---



HOW TO APPLY:



To be considered, please send me the following. Applications without all three items will not be reviewed.



1. A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) describing a Shopify or POD store you've managed before — what you did, what platform, and for how long.



2. Answer this question in 2–3 sentences: A customer
s us saying their hoodie arrived with a faded print. What do you do?



3. Your availability: how many hours per week, which days, and what time zone.



---



DETAILS:



Rate: $5–7/hr depending on experience

Hours: 10–15 hrs/week to start

Schedule: Flexible, with some overlap with US Central Time preferred

Trial: Paid 3-day trial task before full commitment



I respond to every application that follows the instructions above. Looking forward to hearing from you.



— Ricco

ThrivingMoms

title: Shopify Store Builder (Ecom VA – Templates, Liquid, Fast Execution)
description:
Shopify Store Builder / E-Commerce VA with basic coding knowledge who can take existing templates and quickly turn them into new product pages for testing. This role pays $300–$500/month part-time.



About the Role:



This company is a direct-to-consumer brand that launches and tests products quickly. They are looking for someone who can take an existing Shopify product page or landing page template and duplicate it for new products, new angles, and new tests without needing constant hand-holding. This is not a full custom design role. This is an execution role for someone fast, detail-oriented, and comfortable working inside Shopify themes.



What You Will Be Doing:



Your main job is to take existing product page templates and replace copy, swap sections, add new sections when needed, upload products, create variants, and make the basic front-end edits required to get new tests live quickly. You should understand how Shopify product pages are structured and know how to work with templates, sections, custom liquid, and basic theme files.



Responsibilities:

Duplicate and adapt existing Shopify product page templates for new products and new angles

Replace copy, images, headlines, offers, and product information accurately

Upload new products and create product variants properly

Add, remove, and reorder sections based on page needs

Add custom liquid sections when needed

Make basic edits to Shopify theme files and JSON templates

Use AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude to help speed up execution, troubleshoot code, and build sections faster

Handle basic e-commerce tasks inside Shopify without constant supervision

Requirements:

Strong working knowledge of Shopify backend

Basic knowledge of coding, especially Liquid, HTML, CSS, and basic Shopify theme structure

Comfortable editing custom liquid and JSON template files

Understands products, variants, landing pages, and basic direct-response e-commerce structure

Knows how to move fast and follow existing page structures instead of reinventing everything

Comfortable using ChatGPT, Claude, and other AI tools to assist with coding and implementation

Tools You Will Be Using:

Shopify

Liquid

HTML / CSS

JSON templates

ChatGPT / Claude

Google Drive



Send me examples of Shopify pages you have built or worked on, along with a short explanation of what you specifically handled.

title: Opportunity Scout
description:
***If you do not follow directions, your application will not be considered***



Opportunity Scout

Conquer Learning Company  ·  Remote  ·  Part-Time / Contract



Research Outreach Speaking Vendor Opportunities



About the role



Conquer Learning Company is on a mission to equip leaders and organizations to thrive. We are looking for a sharp, resourceful Opportunity Scout to help our CEO get in front of the right rooms, right stages, and right markets.



This is a research-and-outreach role. You will spend your time hunting for speaking engagements and vendor/vending opportunities that are a strong fit for our CEO, then surfacing them in an organized, actionable way.



What you will do



Search the web consistently for speaking engagements, conferences, summits, podcasts, panels, and events that align with our CEO's brand and message



Identify vending and vendor opportunities at markets, expos, festivals, and community events where our products and services can be showcased



Compile and organize your findings into a clean, easy-to-review format



Track deadlines, submission windows, and application requirements for each opportunity



Bring a strategic eye, knowing which opportunities are worth pursuing and which to pass on





Who you are



A natural researcher who loves digging and does not stop at the first page of results



Organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage a running list of opportunities without dropping the ball



Familiar with the education, leadership, faith, and community development spaces (a plus, not required)



Self-directed: you take initiative, ask good questions, and do not need to be micromanaged



A strong communicator who can present findings clearly and concisely



This is a great fit if you love the hunt.



You are the type of person who finds it genuinely satisfying to track down the right opportunity, organize the intel, and hand it off knowing it is exactly what was needed. If that sounds like you, we want to hear from you.



How to Apply



We do not want a resume. We want to hear your voice.



Send us a ***VOICE*** message that covers three things:



Who is Na'Kole Watson? Tell us what you know about her.



What does she do and who does she serve?



What types of speaking opportunities would you look for on her behalf, and why?



Your voice message is your application. It shows us how you think, whether you did your homework, and whether you are the right fit for this role. We look forward to hearing from you.

title: SMS Marketer– Land Investing
description:
BT LAND GROUP

Job Description



Position: SMS Marketer – Land Investing

Reports To: Owner

Location: Remote (Virtual Assistant)

Schedule: Part-Time to Start (20–30 hrs/wk), Full-Time Potential

Probationary Period: 60 Days



About BT Land Group



BT Land Group is a real estate land investment company specializing in the acquisition and resale of vacant land across most U.S. mainland states. We purchase raw, off-market land at a discount—residential, agricultural, and recreational properties outside of city limits—take title, and list it on the MLS with a realtor for immediate resale. Our acquisitions pipeline is powered by high-volume SMS outreach, data-driven targeting, and real conversations with landowners. We're growing fast and building a team of sharp, motivated people who want to grow with us.



Role Overview



The SMS Marketer is the tip of the spear in our acquisitions pipeline. You'll execute high-volume text campaigns through Smarter Contacts to engage landowners, spark real conversations, qualify interest, and move warm leads into Stride CRM for the team to act on. This is not a "blast and forget" role—you'll be thinking on your feet, answering seller questions in real time, and identifying which leads are worth pursuing. We strongly prefer candidates with cold calling or outbound sales experience, because that conversational instinct and ability to handle objections translates directly into being an effective texter.



Key Responsibilities



- Execute and manage targeted SMS marketing campaigns through Smarter Contacts focused on vacant land acquisition across multiple U.S. markets.

- Respond to inbound seller text conversations promptly and professionally—engage naturally and conversationally, not robotically.

- Conduct intuitive exchanges with landowners to uncover motivation, pricing expectations, property details, timeline, and any concerns about selling.

- Qualify leads against BT Land Group's buy box criteria (market value, zoning, acreage, buildability, location, access) and flag strong acquisition opportunities for the team.

- Coordinate with the Lead Manager to route inbound calls that come to the texting number—we do not accept live calls on the texting line, so these must be handed off promptly.

- Perform basic comping and preliminary due diligence on land parcels using Land ID and supporting tools (training provided where needed).

- Log all conversations, notes, and lead details accurately in Stride CRM to maintain a clean, up-to-date pipeline.

- Collaborate with leadership to refine messaging templates, targeting criteria, and overall SMS strategy based on real performance data.

- Track and report on key performance metrics including response rates, lead quality, and conversion to help continuously improve campaign results.

- Maintain organized follow-up workflows to keep warm leads engaged and moving through the pipeline over time.



Requirements



- Prior experience in SMS marketing, cold calling, or outbound sales—ideally in real estate or a related industry (strongly preferred).

- Strong written communication skills with the ability to build rapport and trust through text-based conversation.

- Quick on your feet—comfortable answering unexpected seller questions and handling pushback in real time via text.

- Analytical mindset with the ability to recognize promising deals versus red flags based on property details and seller motivation.

- Highly organized with strong time-management skills; able to juggle multiple active conversations and campaigns simultaneously.

- Self-starter who thrives working independently in a remote environment while staying aligned with team goals and priorities.

- Comfortable learning and adapting to new tools and platforms quickly.

- Availability during U.S. business hours for real-time seller engagement.

-bonus if you can work weekends 



Preferred Qualifications



- Experience using Smarter Contacts, Launch Control, or similar SMS/outreach platforms.

- Familiarity with land investing, land values, zoning, road access, utilities, and rural property considerations.

- Experience with CRM systems (Stride, Podio, REsimpli, or similar) and lead management workflows.

- Prior experience working with motivated seller leads or on an acquisitions team in real estate investing.



Compensation & Benefits



- Competitive hourly compensation based on experience and skill level.

- Quarterly performance-based bonus potential tied to lead quality, responsiveness, and contribution to successful acquisitions.

- Starts part-time (20–30 hours/week) with a 60-day probationary period. Strong performers will have the opportunity to transition to full-time (40 hours/week).

- Hands-on training and development in land investing, acquisitions strategy, and SMS marketing systems.

- Clear path for pay growth and expanded responsibilities as the company scales.



How to Apply



Interested candidates should submit their resume along with a brief message covering the following:



- Your relevant experience in SMS marketing, cold calling, or outbound sales.

- Why you're interested in land investing and this role specifically.

- A recording of yourself on a call. If you don't have an existing recording, please send a short clip of yourself introducing yourself as if you were speaking to a potential seller for the first time.

- Your availability (hours per week and time zone).

- Your favorite food.

title: Inventory Buyer (Amazon FBA)
description:
Inventory Buyer (Amazon FBA) — Performance-Based Role



We are looking for a disciplined, data-driven Inventory Buyer responsible for deploying capital into profitable inventory opportunities across Amazon FBA.



Leads will be provided to you. 



This is not a sourcing role. This is a decision-making role.



Your job is not to find products—it is to filter, validate, and execute high-quality purchasing decisions that generate consistent profit and fast inventory turnover.



Application Process (Read Carefully)



To be considered for this role, you must complete our Inventory Buyer Qualification Quiz.



Only candidates who demonstrate strong decision-making ability will be contacted

If you do not pass, you will not receive a response

Qualified candidates will be contacted by our team to schedule an interview



Please read the rest of this posting before taking the exam: 



This is intentional. This role requires precision, and the quiz is designed to reflect real decision-making scenarios.



Who This Role Is (and Is NOT) For



This role is strictly for experienced operators.



This is not for beginners

This is not for those looking to “learn” or “try out” inventory buying

This is not a training position



This is for individuals who:



Have real experience purchasing inventory for resale

Understand how to evaluate deals using data (Keepa, SellerAmp, etc.)

Can handle the responsibility of deploying capital and making independent decisions



If you do not already have experience in this area, this role will not be a fit.



Core Responsibility



You will evaluate potential inventory opportunities and d
 what to buy using strict criteria around:



Profitability

Sales velocity

Price stability

Competition

Account health risk



Every decision directly impacts company capital. You are expected to operate with precision, discipline, and accountability.



What Success Looks Like

Inventory consistently sells within 30–60 days

Purchases meet or exceed 35% ROI

High percentage of buys convert into profitable outcomes (70–80%+ success rate)

Minimal capital tied up in slow or dead inventory

Clean, compliant supply chain with proper documentation

Key Responsibilities

Review and analyze sourcing leads daily

Apply strict BUY / NO BUY criteria to every opportunity

Execute test buys and scale winners responsibly

Manage restocking decisions based on real sales data

Monitor inventory performance and adjust strategy

Maintain organized records of invoices and suppliers

Ensure all purchases meet Amazon account health and compliance standards

Performance Metrics



You will be evaluated based on:



Net profit generated

Sell-through rate (target: 70%+ within 60 days)

ROI on purchases (minimum 35%)

Decision accuracy (% of profitable buys)

Capital efficiency (profit per dollar deployed)

Compensation Structure



Base: $150/month - $400/mo

Commission: % of net profit on SOLD inventory (tiered based on performance)

Speed Bonus:

Sold within 30 days ? additional bonus

Sold within 60 days ? smaller bonus



Penalty:

Inventory that does not sell within 90–120 days or is liquidated earns zero commission



You are paid based on results, not activity.



Workflow & Training



Phase 1 (First 2–3 Weeks):



Daily review of all purchase decisions

Real-time feedback and correction



Phase 2 (30–60 Days):



Controlled autonomy with budget limits per decision



Phase 3 (60+ Days):



Full autonomy within system rules



Performance reviewed through metrics, not supervision



Non-Negotiable Standards

You must be able to confidently say NO to 90% of opportunities

No emotional or speculative buying

No exceptions to criteria

No shortcuts on compliance or documentation

Required Skills & Experience



Extremely Important: 

Must have a STRONG understanding of Amazon FBA ecosystem, account health and invoice/supplier requirements

Experience with Keepa, SellerAmp, or similar tools

Ability to read and interpret sales data and pricing trends

Knowledge of Amazon account health, IP claims, and invoice requirements

High attention to detail and organization



Who This Is For



This role is for someone who:



Thinks in terms of ROI and capital allocation, not tasks

Is disciplined and process-driven

Prefers making fewer, high-quality decisions over high volume

Wants to be rewarded directly based on performance



Who This Is NOT For

Anyone without prior inventory purchasing experience

Anyone looking to learn on the job

Anyone who needs constant supervision

Anyone who prioritizes volume over quality

Anyone who makes decisions based on assumptions instead of data



Final Note



This role directly controls capital.



If you make strong decisions, you will scale your income alongside the business.

If you make poor decisions, it will show immediately in the numbers.



Start by completing the qualification quiz. Only top performers will move forward.



Inventory Purchasing Quiz:

title: ???? Client Success & Operations Coordinator — Level Next Consulting & Marketing
description:
Location: Remote (Philippines) | Type: Full-Time | Hours: M–F, overlap with US Pacific Time (PST) preferred



About Us

Level Next Consulting & Marketing is a fast-growing digital marketing agency based in San Diego, California. We build omnichannel authority marketing systems for service-based businesses — coaches, consultants, attorneys, lenders, real estate professionals, and more — helping them dominate search, social, and AI-powered recommendations without hiring an entire in-house team.

Our services include SEO, AEO (Answer Engine Optimization), social media management, content creation, video production, paid advertising, web development, and monthly client reporting.

We currently serve 8 active clients across multiple industries with a fully remote team, and we are growing quickly. Our team includes:



Max — CEO & Owner. Runs strategy, sales, oversees all client marketing, and leads the business.

Ephraim — Social media scheduling, blog publishing, reel posting

Faye — Graphic design, carousels, ad graphics, operations support

Shao — Graphic design, social media, admin

Jade — Website optimization & digital marketing

Philip — Web development

Arma — Video editing



We are a lean, execution-focused team that cares deeply about client results. Our culture is direct, fast-moving, and built on accountability and trust.



The Role

We are hiring a Client Success & Operations Coordinator — someone who becomes the operational backbone of Level Next and, in many ways, a second voice for Max with clients and the team.

This is not a purely administrative role. This person is client-facing, team-facing, and systems-focused. You will be the person who makes sure the right things get done by the right people at the right time — and that our clients always feel informed, taken care of, and confident in us.

As we scale, this role will grow into a fractional agency operations manager — improving our systems, refining our workflows, and ensuring Level Next operates like a well-oiled machine even as we add more clients and tea
mbers.



What You'll Be Responsible For

Client Onboarding & Information Gathering



Reach out to new clients via
 and coordinate the collection of all necessary brand and account details

Collect and securely store: brand guidelines, logo files, brand voice docs, social media credentials (Instagram,
, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube), website login access (WordPress, Wix, Squarespace, etc.), Google Business Profile access, Google Analytics, ad account access, and any relevant CRM credentials

Build and maintain a Client Intake Tracker so nothing falls through the cracks

Set the tone for the client relationship from day one — professional, warm, organized



Client Communication & Relationship Management



Serve as a consistent point of contact for clients when Max is unavailable

Respond to client questions, update requests, and status inquiries in a timely, professional manner

Keep clients informed about what's being worked on, what's coming up, and when to expect deliverables

Flag anything urgent or sensitive to Max immediately

Manage communication across
, Slack, and any client communication channels



Team Accountability & Deliverable Tracking



Own our Master Content Calendar — a Google Sheets document covering 8 active clients with 40+ recurring weekly deliverables across blog posts, reels, carousels,
s, podcasts, social posts, and graphic design assets

Know what every tea
mber (Ephraim, Faye, Shao, Jade, Philip, Arma) is responsible for each week and hold them accountable to deadlines

Follow up proactively when deliverables are late or at risk

Keep the Asana project management system updated — tasks marked complete, new tasks created, due dates accurate

Run a weekly team check-in or async status update to confirm progress across all clients



Current Weekly Deliverables You'll Be Tracking

Our active client deliverables include (but are not limited to):

Agent Elite — Daily reels (podcast clips + product/service), weekly blog, weekly carousel, weekly stories, weekly podcast shot/edited/published to YouTube and streaming platforms

LMFCE — Bi-weekly blogs (two series), bi-weekly
 blasts, bi-weekly carousel, on-demand graphic design

Saffron & Sage — Daily reels (Opus Clip + Studio Luniste), weekly blog, weekly marketing
s, monthly newsletter, 3 weekly carousels, reel covers for every reel

InVivo Leadership Strategies — Weekly podcast shot/edited/published, daily podcast reels, 3 weekly reels (Mon/Wed/Fri), monthly newsletter, weekly marketing
s, reel covers

Panoramic Lending — Bi-weekly podcast/long-form video, daily reels, reel covers, monthly newsletter, weekly marketing
s, 3 weekly carousels (Mon/Wed/Fri), weekly blog

Yost Quesada Team — On-demand blogs, HubSpot
 templates, LinkedIn + YouTube Shorts posting

CQ2K — Weekly blog, monthly newsletter

Timeshare Counsel — Growing account; deliverables being confirmed

Each deliverable has a defined owner, process, storage location in Google Drive, and posting workflow. You will own ensuring each of these moves through the pipeline correctly and on time.

Systems Improvement & Operations



As you get comfortable in the role, identify gaps, bottlenecks, and inefficiencies in our workflows

Propose and help implement improvements to how we track work, communicate, and deliver for clients

Help document SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) so the team always knows exactly how to execute recurring tasks

Support Max in building a business that can scale without everything running through him





Tools You'll Be Working In



Asana — Project and task management for all clients

Google Drive — Asset storage and organization (client-specific folder structures)

Google Sheets — Master Content Calendar and tracking documents

Slack — Internal team communication

Gmail /
 — Client communication

Vista Social — Social media scheduling (awareness/familiarity is a plus)

GoHighLevel / RAD CRM —
 marketing platforms (training provided)

Notion or Google Docs — SOPs and documentation





Who You Are



You are exceptionally organized — the kind of person who can track 10 moving parts across 8 clients and never drop a ball

You are an excellent written communicator — professional in English, warm with clients, clear with the team

You have sharp follow-through — you don't just know what's due, you make sure it gets done

You are proactive, not reactive — you see problems before they become fires and act on them

You are comfortable being client-facing — you can represent Level Next professionally with confidence

You are systems-minded — you naturally look for ways to make things run smoother

You have experience working with a US-based agency, marketing team, or service business (strongly preferred)

You have worked in a project coordination, account management, virtual assistant, or operations role — ideally in a marketing or creative agency context

You are available for meaningful overlap with US Pacific Time (PST) — ideally at least 4–6 hours per day





What This Is NOT



This is not a passive task-list role. You will need to think, communicate, and problem-solve.

This is not a graphic design or video editing role (we have tea
mbers for that).

This is not a role for someone who waits to be told what to do — we need someone who takes ownership.





Compensation & Growth



Competitive salary based on experience (please include your expected monthly rate in your application)

This role has clear growth potential — as Level Next scales, so does this position

You will have direct access to the CEO and be a trusted part of a tight-knit, high-performing team

You will learn a tremendous amount about modern digital marketing, AI marketing systems, and agency operations





How to Apply

Please send the following to 
 with the subject line: "CS Ops Coordinator – [Your Name]"



A short introduction about yourself and your relevant experience (3–5 sentences)

Your available working hours and PST overlap

One example of a time you managed multiple deadlines or kept a team accountable — what happened, what you did, and what the outcome was

Any relevant tools you've used (Asana, Slack, Google Workspace, CRMs, etc.)



Applications without the subject line format will not be reviewed.

We respond to every qualified applicant. Looking forward to meeting you.

— Max Bergstrom, CEO | Level Next Consulting & Marketing

title: Virtual Assistant — Youth Events & Leadership Brand (Social Media + Admin)
description:
Virtual Assistant — Youth Events & Leadership Brand

PART-TIME · ONGOING · REMOTE · PHILIPPINES-BASED

?? Please Read Before Applying



This is NOT an e-commerce, dropshipping, or product-based brand. We do not sell physical products, run a Shopify store, or manage inventory. If your VA experience is primarily in e-commerce or online retail, this role is not the right fit for you. Please do not apply.



We are looking for a VA with experience supporting service-based businesses, events companies, educational brands, coaching brands, or community-focused organizations.



Who We Are



The Elevated Youth Experience LLC is a premium youth brand based in the United States. We create high-end events, leadership development programs, creative workshops, and curated experiences for children ages 6–11 and middle school youth.



We are not a daycare. We are not a party planning company. We are a movement-driven brand that blends leadership development, emotional intelligence, and premium celebration into one experience. Think of us as the intersection of a high-end event brand and a youth empowerment organization.



Our brand is bold, colorful, premium, and deeply intentional. Everything we do is curated. Everything we communicate has to reflect that standard.



What You Will Do



Manage and schedule Instagram and
 content using 
 templates — 5 posts per week

Write captions in our brand voice — premium, bold, warm, and emotionally intelligent. No generic content. No product language. No shop links.

Respond to DMs and comments professionally and in brand voice within 24 hours

Research and engage with target audiences — parents, schools, community organizations, and family-focused brands

Maintain and update our Squarespace website with events, program info, and photos

Build and manage our parent
 list and send weekly newsletters via Mailchimp

Research venues, vendors, and local businesses for events and sponsorship opportunities

Organize all brand files, photos, and documents in Google Drive

Send a daily morning check-in and end-of-day summary — the founder works a full-time job and needs clean, efficient updates

What We Are Looking For



Experience supporting a service business, events brand, coaching brand, nonprofit, or educational organization — not a product store

Excellent written English with a natural ability to write warmly, boldly, and professionally

Strong Canva skills — you must be able to create premium-looking social media graphics with no training

Experience with Instagram and
 content management — not just posting, but engaging, growing, and representing a brand

Comfortable working independently for 8+ hours with minimal check-ins from the founder

Highly organized, self-directed, and proactive — you bring solutions, not just questions

Some experience with website updates (Squarespace preferred) and
 marketing (Mailchimp or similar)

Passionate about children, education, empowerment, or premium service brands

Available during some US Eastern Time hours for real-time communication

Hours & Compensation



This role starts at 10–15 hours per week at $5–6 USD per hour depending on experience and skill level. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with this brand long-term. As the brand grows, your hours and pay will grow with it. We reward loyalty, quality, and initiative.



How To Apply



Send a message that includes all of the following. Incomplete applications will not receive a response.



A brief introduction — who you are and what kind of brands you have supported in the past

Your Canva skill level on a scale of 1–10 and what you can create without training

One Instagram caption written right now, in your own words, for this brand. Our program is called The Elevated Youth Leadership Series — a 6-week leadership program for middle school students. Write the caption as if you already work here. Do not use AI.

title: Experience Email Marketer
description:
ABOUT DISPENZA

Dispenza has quickly become one of the largest marketing agencies in the rapidly-growing emerging market of cannabis as it quickly legalizes state by state in the Americas. As the market matures, we are on a mission to help change how the market operates, leaving the more questionable activities done by current market leaders and emerge as the first marketing agency and platform that values our customers while simultaneously delivering business-changing results to our clients.



JOB OVERVIEW

We’re looking for an experience
 Marketer to support ongoing campaign execution, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategy. This role focuses on building and optimizing
 campaigns, automations, SMS, and Wallet/ePass messaging to drive engagement, retention, and revenue.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

- Plan, build, and execute
 marketing campaigns, including one-off broadcasts and automated lifecycle flows

- Develop and manage automations such as welcome series, abandoned cart, re-engagement, and retention sequences

- Segment audiences and implement targeted messaging strategies based on customer behavior and lifecycle stage

- Design and run A/B tests on subject lines, content, timing, and campaign structure to improve performance

- Monitor, analyze, and report on campaign performance (open rates, CTR, conversions, and revenue attribution)

- Translate performance data into actionable insights and optimization strategies

- Create clear, concise performance reports with recommendations for continuous improvement

- Support Wallet/ePass and SMS campaign initiatives as part of a broader retention strategy

- Ensure all campaigns are aligned with brand voice, compliance standards, and business goals



TEAM COLLABORATION

- Work closely with project managers, designers, and stakeholders to execute campaigns efficiently

- Participate in team reviews to evaluate performance and identify opportunities for improvement

- Communicate updates proactively and flag blockers, risks, or optimization opportunities early



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 3–5 years of experience in
 marketing

- Hands-on experience with platforms such as Klaviyo, HubSpot, Mailchimp, or Salesforce

- Proven track record managing high-volume
 lists and driving measurable revenue impact

- Strong understanding of segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and customer journey mapping

- Experience building and optimizing high-performing campaigns and automations

- Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and translate it into actionable strategies

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable

- Strong communication skills with the ability to provide clear updates and ask thoughtful questions



DESIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- Experience with retention marketing in eCommerce or DTC brands

- Familiarity with SMS and/or Wallet (ePass) marketing campaigns

- Basic design awareness and experience working with templates or tools like Figma or Canva

- Experience in regulated industries (e.g., cannabis is a plus, but not required)



COMPENSATION

$500-$900 starting depending on experience.



SCHEDULE

- 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, 7:00am to 4:00pm Philippine Time.

- Minimal overtime during weekdays and weekends will be expected as part of salary



Dispenza is proud to be an equal opportunity employer committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, wordpressage, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other basis protected by law.



HOW TO APPLY

title: Creative Strategist (Meta Ads / UGC)
description:
Creative Strategist (Meta Ads & UGC)



We’re hiring a Creative Strategist (Meta Ads & UGC)  to help us scale our paid creative output for one of our ecommerce brands.



This role sits between creative strategy and execution. You will research winning ad concepts, identify ideas worth testing for our brand, adapt and write scripts, prepare voiceover and soundtrack drafts, create clear briefs for editors, and coordinate creator content production.



We’re looking for someone with strong creative taste, Meta ad understanding, good written English, and solid execution skills.



What You’ll Own



1. Creative Research & Strategy Support



You will research competitor and adjacent-brand ads, identify concepts and scripts that could work for our brand, and help build a clear pipeline of ideas worth testing. You should be able to recognize strong hooks, understand what makes an ad compelling, and think in terms of “same but different” rather than just copying what already exists.



2. Script Development



You will adapt winning concepts to our brand, write new scripts when needed, and create multiple hook variations for each concept. You should understand how to write for paid social, especially Meta, and know the difference between writing something that sounds good and writing something that is designed to convert.



3. Creative Assembly & Briefing



You will generate voiceovers, pair them with music, and assemble rough audio drafts that can be used for production. You will also turn concepts into clear, structured Notion briefs for editors, including line-by-line shot guidance, references, and relevant asset links. You should have enough editing sense to understand rhythm, pacing, and how a creative should come together.



4. Creator Coordination



You will help source creators for content batches following our SOPs, support outreach and follow-up, keep communication organized, and make sure deliverables are collected and stored properly. You should be comfortable coordinating moving parts, tracking progress, and helping maintain a reliable flow of fresh creator content.



Expected Deliverables :



The role is designed to support a consistent paid creative testing pipeline by following SOPs. The expected output includes:



- Consistent production of new video briefs (8 per month to start)

- Script development, voiceover generation, ongoing research

- 1 creator batch every 1–2 months, typically around 5 creators

- Creative Strategy research



What We’re Looking For



You are likely a strong fit if you have experience working on Meta creatives for ecommerce brands and understand how paid social ads are structured to perform.



You should have:



- Experience with Meta /
 ad creatives

- strong understanding of hooks, angles, and direct-response creative

- good written English

- the ability to identify winning concepts and adapt them to a brand

- basic to strong editing skills

- Experience in following SOPs

- a good sense of rhythm, pacing, and creative flow

- strong organization and attention to detail

- the ability to turn ideas into clean, actionable briefs

- experience in DTC / ecommerce



Nice to Have



experience using DaVinci Resolve

experience with 11Labs or other voiceover tools

experience working with UGC creators / influencer coordination

experience building briefs in Notion



Payment



This role will start part-time (4 days a week, Monday to Thursday) moving toward full-time.

Compensation will depend on experience and proof of past results, but our initial target range is to start around $8/hour - $1024/month to start, with room to grow for the right person as results and responsibilities expand.



How to Apply



Please include:

a short note on your experience with Meta ad creatives

examples of ecommerce creative work you’ve been involved in

any relevant scriptwriting, creative strategy, editing, or briefing experience

tools you’ve used (for example: DaVinci, 11Labs, Notion, etc.)



BrandJet I Propelling Brands into Space

brandjet.eu

title: Nonprofit Grant Writer & Proposal Assistant
description:
I’m looking for a part-time Nonprofit Grant Writer & Proposal Assistant to support grant writing and proposal development for my nonprofit clients.



This role is ideal for someone who is strong in grant writing, proposal development, and written communication, and who can also support related tasks such as reviewing guidelines, drafting reports, adapting content for different funders, and keeping proposal materials organized and on track.



The primary focus of this role is helping move grant applications forward from outline to draft to submission support. I’m looking for someone who can work independently with background materials, previous proposals, funder guidelines, and strategic direction, without needing step-by-step instruction for every task.



About the Business

I run a fundraising consulting business that supports nonprofit organizations with strategy, campaigns, and systems. A key part of my work includes helping clients develop strong grant applications and manage the writing process in a clear, organized, and professional way.



I’m looking for someone who can support that work with strong writing, editing, attention to detail, and a willingness to work within established systems and workflows.



What You’ll Be Doing



 - Review funder guidelines and summarize key application requirements, questions, and deadlines

 - Draft grant proposals, letters of inquiry, reports, and other funder-facing content using source materials I provide

 - Adapt existing content for different funders and application formats

 - Pull key information from client documents, websites, reports, previous proposals, and approved source materials

 - Help organize proposal materials, supporting documents, and standard content

 - Maintain grant trackers, calendars, and application status updates

 - Edit and revise draft content based on feedback

 - Flag missing information, inconsistencies, or questions before submission

 - Support a clear and organized workflow across multiple deadlines and client projects



What Matters Most

Strong writing and editing skills are the most important requirements for this role.



A key aspect of our process is working within an AI-supported writing workflow. Our process uses AI in the preliminary drafting stage, followed by human review, refinement, and adaptation. I’m looking for someone who can use AI efficiently to support early drafts, then apply strong judgment, editing skills, and attention to detail to ensure the final writing is accurate, clear, funder-aligned, and reflective of the client’s voice, program focus, and approved source materials.



Strong written English is essential. Attention to detail is also very important, especially when working across different deadlines, applications, and supporting documents.



Experience with nonprofit grant writing, proposal support, or donor communications is a strong asset.



This Role Is a Good Fit If You:



 - Are confident in your writing, editing, and proofreading skills

 - Can review funder guidelines and pull out the most important details

 - Are comfortable turning background information into strong draft content

 - Can adapt writing for different funders, formats, and audiences

 - Are comfortable using AI as part of a structured writing process

 - Need minimal direction once you understand the goals and workflow

 - Are highly organized and able to manage details across multiple projects

 - Are comfortable working within project management systems like ClickUp and following established workflow templates

 - Catch small mistakes, inconsistencies, and missing pieces before submitting work

 - Communicate clearly and ask thoughtful questions when needed

 - Are interested in a longer-term working relationship



Qualities Needed to Succeed



Integrity

You communicate honestly, take responsibility for your work, and let me know if something is unclear or delayed.



Commitment

I’m looking for someone interested in building a longer-term working relationship, not just completing one quick task.



Desire to Learn

I value someone who is open to feedback, willing to improve, and interested in growing with the role over time.



Attention to Detail

You notice inconsistencies, missing information, formatting issues, and small mistakes before work is submitted.



Good Judgment

You know how to use AI as a support tool, not a substitute for thinking. You review outputs carefully, verify details, and make sure the final work is accurate and appropriate for the client and funder.



About the Role

This is a part-time role to start, with potential to grow over time depending on fit, workflow, and evolving needs.



This role may involve supporting multiple nonprofit clients and projects at the same time, so strong organization and comfort switching between priorities will be important.



To Apply

Use cccVA02 in your subject line. Please begin the first line of your
 application with the words east coast grant.



Please also include:



 - A short note about your experience with grant writing, proposal development, or proposal support

 - One or two writing samples related to grants, proposals, reports, or nonprofit writing

 - A brief note explaining your role in each sample

 - A short description of your process for turning source material into a strong draft

 - A note about your experience using AI to support writing, editing, or proposal development



This is a part-time role to start, with an estimated workload of approximately 15 hours per week. Hours may vary slightly depending on active deadlines and client needs. There is potential for this role to grow over time depending on fit, workflow, and evolving needs.

title: YouTube Video Editor
description:
**Title:** Video Editor — Pop Culture / Anime / Movies YouTube Channel (Long-Form & Short-Form)



**About Us:**



CM Studios is a growing YouTube channel focused on pop culture content — movies, anime, cartoons, TV shows, Star Wars, Marvel, DC, horror, and collectibles. We create both long-form YouTube videos and daily short-form content (Shorts, Reels, TikToks) and are looking for a skilled video editor to join our team and help us scale. We plan for this role to grow as the channel grows.



**What You'll Be Doing:**



- Editing long-form YouTube videos (our priority — at least one quality video per month)

- Editing daily short-form videos (15-60 seconds) for YouTube Shorts, TikTok, and Instagram Reels

- You will receive raw recorded footage and creative direction fro
 — I record all content and will communicate the vision for each video. You can also review the existing channel to understand the style and direction.

- You should have some relative knowledge of the niche. You don't have to be an expert — I am the expert. You can ask questions where needed, but I do expect you to be able to find quality clips, quality SFX, and quality level clips on your own.

- After each video is uploaded, spending approximately 1 hour on community engagement: responding to comments on our content and engaging with related creators' content to boost visibility

- Maintaining a consistent upload schedule and being available during upload windows



**What We're Looking For:**



- Proven experience editing both long-form and short-form content for YouTube

- Must have edited at least one short-form video that reached **1,000,000+ views on YouTube Shorts** or **10,000,000+ views on TikTok** (please include link)

- Must have edited at least one long-form video that reached **100,000+ views on YouTube** (please include link)

- Strong skills in storytelling, pacing, sound design, transitions, and retention editing for long-form YouTube content

- Strong understanding of hooks, text overlays, and trending editing styles for short-form platforms

- Familiarity with pop culture content — movies, anime (Dragon Ball, Naruto, One Piece, etc.), cartoons, TV shows, Star Wars, Marvel/DC, and horror

- Understanding of YouTube algorithm and what makes both long-form and short-form content perform

- Reliable internet connection and ability to communicate clearly in English

- Experience with community engagement and understanding of engagement strategies is a plus



**How You'll Be Paid:**



- **Short-form videos:** Per-video rate, hourly rate, or whatever pricing structure works best for you — just tell us how you prefer to charge for short-form edits

- **Long-form videos:** Hourly rate or per-project rate — tell us your preferred pricing structure

- **Engagement work:** 1 hour of community engagement paid after each upload

- Payment issued upon completion of each video cycle (edit + engagement hour)

- Consistent, ongoing work — this role is designed to grow as the channel grows



**To Apply, Please Include:**



1. A brief introduction about yourself and your editing experience

2. A link to at least one short-form video you edited that reached 1M+ views on YouTube Shorts or 10M+ on TikTok

3. A link to at least one long-form video you edited that reached 100K+ views on YouTube

4. Links to your portfolio or channels you've edited for, showing both long-form and short-form work

5. Your pricing: how you charge for short-form edits and your hourly or per-project rate for long-form

6. Review our content across all platforms (links below) and answer this question: **Pick one of our existing videos (long-form or short-form) and explain specifically how you would have edited it differently to get more views.** Be specific — mention the hook, pacing, storytelling, retention, transitions, or any changes you'd make.



**Our Channels (please review before applying):**

- YouTube: 

- TikTok: 

- Instagram: 

- Letterboxd: 



**Trial Period:**



Selected candidates will be given a paid trial — editing one 15-20 second test video to evaluate fit, style, and quality before moving forward.



**Important:** To confirm you've read this entire post carefully, please include the phrase **"the iron giant"** somewhere in your response.

title: Anti-Bot & Browser Fingerprinting Specialist — Web Automation (Remote)
description:
ABOUT US:

VisaBridge is a licensed travel agency specializing in Portuguese visa appointment automation. We currently operate a fully functional automation system (Python + Playwright) that processes
appointments every Friday. Our code works, but the embassy's anti-bot detection is constantly evolving. We need a specialist who can keep our system INVISIBLE to server-side detection — permanently.



THE REALITY:

Every Friday at 11:40 AM Lisbon time,
appointment slots are released and fully booked within 1-5 MINUTES. Our competitors use elite-level automation with advanced anti-detection techniques. The embassy is actively investing in bot detection technology. Any system that is identified as a bot gets blocked immediately — resulting in zero appointments for that session. This is a constant arms race, and we intend to WIN it.



YOUR MISSION:

You will analyze the embassy's anti-bot detection mechanisms and ensure our automation system passes ALL detection checks. You will also be on standby during booking windows to deploy countermeasures in real-time if the embassy updates their detection.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

• Analyze the target server's bot detection — identify exactly what checks are performed (fingerprinting, header analysis, TLS fingerprint, behavioral analysis, rate limiting)

• Implement browser fingerprint spoofing — Canvas, WebGL, AudioContext, fonts, hardware concurrency, screen resolution, plugins, navigator properties

• Manipulate TLS fingerprints (JA3/JA4) to match genuine Chrome browser traffic

• Build dynamic header injection — headers must vary realistically per request, not be static

• Implement cookie/session persistence across booking cycles to reduce login frequency

• Build User-Agent rotation with CONSISTENT header sets (UA + sec-ch-ua + Accept + Accept-Language must all match)

• Monitor embassy website changes and deploy detection countermeasures BEFORE competitors adapt

• Stay on standby every Friday during booking window (6:50 PM - 9:00 PM PH time) to deploy emergency fixes



CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:

The embassy website and anti-bot systems change WITHOUT NOTICE. You MUST be available to analyze changes and deploy fixes IMMEDIATELY — especially on Fridays during the booking window. A 30-minute delay means losing thousands of euros. This is non-negotiable.



REQUIREMENTS:

• 4+ years experience in web scraping and browser automation

• Deep expertise in browser fingerprinting techniques

• Experience with TLS fingerprint manipulation (JA3/JA4)

• Strong knowledge of HTTP protocols, headers, and browser behavior

• Experience with network analysis tools (Wireshark, Charles Proxy)

• Python + Playwright advanced level

• Experience bypassing bot detection systems (Cloudflare, DataDome, PerimeterX, or similar)

• Ability to reverse-engineer anti-bot mechanisms from network traffic analysis

• Fast turnaround on detection countermeasures

• Stable internet connection (minimum 20 Mbps)



COMPENSATION:

• $1,200 - $2,500/month depending on experience level

• Performance bonus: $
/month based on detection evasion success rate

• Long-term position — this is an ongoing arms race, not a one-time project



HOW TO APPLY:

Send your resume along with:

1. Description of bot detection systems you have bypassed (with specific techniques used)

2. Your approach to browser fingerprint spoofing (tools, techniques, results)

3. How you would analyze a server's detection mechanism from scratch

4. Your available start date (earlier = better — competition is getting stronger every week)

title: Sales Closer — Help US Small Businesses Get Online ($10/hr + $50 Per Sale)
description:
About Us:                                                                                                                                             

  We're Typesite — we use AI to build custom websites for small businesses in under 30 minutes. Our product is live, we have paying customers, and we need someone to help us grow by reaching business owners who don't have a website yet.                                                                

                                                                                                                                                        

  The Role:

  You'll be calling US-based small business owners (landscapers, contractors, cleaning companies, etc.) who we've already identified as not having a website. This is a two-call process:                                                                                                                  

   

  Call 1 — The Easy Call:                                                                                                                               

  You offer to build them a free custom website. No catch, no credit card. You ask about their business, collect some basic info, and we build their site using AI in about 30 minutes.                                                                                                                    

   

  Call 2 — The Close:                                                                                                                                   

  You call them back, walk them through their finished website, and close them on a $50/month hosting subscription. They're looking at a real site with their business name, photos, and services already on it. You're not selling an idea — you're showing them something that's already built for them.    

   

  Compensation:                                                                                                                                         

  - $8-10/hr base (depending on experience)                       

  - $50 commission for every new paying subscriber you close                                                                                            

  - Top performers can realistically earn $3,000-4,000+ per month

                                                                                                                                                        

  Schedule:                                                                                                                                             

  - 30 hours/week (flexible within US business hours, roughly 8am-5pm EST)

  - Long-term ongoing role, not a short project                                                                                                         

                                                                  

  What We Provide:                                                                                                                                      

  - Pre-built lead lists of businesses without websites (you're not hunting for leads)

  - A product that builds itself in 30 minutes (you don't need to know anything technical)                                                              

  - A free offer for the first call that makes the conversation easy                      

  - Training on the product and the close                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                        

  You're a Great Fit If You:                                                                                                                            

  - Have experience calling US-based customers and are comfortable with American conversational style                                                   

  - Sound natural and warm on the phone — not robotic or scripted                                                                                       

  - Have done cold calling, appointment setting, or phone sales before                                                                                  

  - Can manage your own callback schedule without being micromanaged                                                                                    

  - Speak clear, confident English                                                                                                                      

  - Get excited about commission because you know you can close                                                                                         

                                                                                                                                                        

  How to Apply:                                                                                                                                         

  Record a 60-second voice note introducing yourself as if you're calling a landscaping business owner to offer them a free website. Don't read a script

   — just be yourself. Send it with your application. If I enjoy the conversation, we'll talk.

title: Credit Repair Sales Agent
description:
We are seeking an experienced Credit Repair Sales Agent whose primary responsibility is to convert leads into enrolled clients.



This is a sales-only position. You will not be handling disputes, backend processing, or account servicing.



Your role is to speak with inbound leads, identify their needs, present our services, and close them into our program. This position requires urgency, confidence, and the ability to consistently convert prospects into paying clients.



Key Responsibilities

 • Contact new leads immediately as they enter the pipeline

 • Conduct consultation calls to assess the prospect’s credit situation

 • Lead enrollment calls and guide clients into the program

 • Follow up with leads through calls, SMS, and CRM communication

 • Handle objections and confidently ask for the sale

 • Accurately document all interactions in GoHighLevel CRM

 • Maintain consistent communication with management



Compensation

 • Hourly pay based on experience

 • Commission per enrollment

 • Performance-based bonuses available



This is a performance-driven role with strong earning potential for the right candidate.

Minimum Requirements (Do Not Apply If You Do Not Meet These)

 • At least 1 year of credit repair experience

 • At least 2 years of sales experience (phone sales preferred)

 • Strong understanding of:

 • Credit reports

 • Collections, charge-offs, and late payments

 • Credit-building strategies

 • Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written)

 • Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment

 • Ability to work U.S. business hours

Ideal Candidate



The ideal candidate is:

 • Highly responsive and able to act quickly on new leads

 • Confident and in control of conversations

 • Results-driven and motivated by commission

 • Organized and detail-oriented

 • Professional in tone and communication

 • Consistent with follow-up and lead management

Performance Expectations

 • Contact leads within minutes of receiving them

 • Build rapport and clearly identify the prospect’s needs

 • Present the service in a structured and confident manner

 • Consistently convert leads into enrollments



Why Join Our Team

 • Consistent flow of inbound leads

 • Established brand with a strong reputation

 • Proven systems and structured sales process

 • Opportunity for growth and increased earnings

Important



This is not an entry-level position. We are looking for someone who already understands both credit repair and sales and can perform without constant supervision.

title: Virtual Portfolio or Property Manager – Short-Term Rentals
description:
We are still expanding and need more people to join our team!



At Lodgo, we deliver innovative, efficient property management solutions that help property owners maximize performance and revenue. Our team combines advanced software, smart systems, and data-driven strategies to provide seamless operations and outstanding guest experiences.



We are looking for a highly organized, guest-focused Virtual Portfolio Manager to oversee a growing portfolio of 50+ short-term rental properties. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes ownership of their work, and is committed to delivering consistent 5-star hospitality.



Key Responsibilities



Guest Communication

- Respond promptly and professionally to inquiries across Airbnb, 
, Vrbo, and other platforms

- Maintain a friendly, solution-oriented tone at all times



Reservation Management

- Confirm bookings and monitor availability

- Manage modifications, cancellations, and special requests

- Coordinate with hosts when necessary



Issue Resolution

- Address guest concerns quickly and effectively

- Escalate maintenance or cleaning issues to the appropriate teams

- Ensure timely follow-up until full resolution



Reputation Management

- Dispute inaccurate or unfair reviews when necessary

- Follow up with guests after checkout

- Encourage positive feedback and repeat bookings



Pre- and Post-Stay Support

- Send detailed check-in instructions and house rules

- Provide curated local recommendations

- Address post-stay feedback and concerns



Case & Ticket Management

- Handle Airbnb support cases efficiently

- Create and monitor tickets for maintenance, cleaning, and operational tasks

- Track completion and ensure service standards are met



Team Collaboration

- Work closely with cleaning and maintenance teams

- Ensure smooth turnovers and fast problem resolution



Data & Performance Tracking

- Monitor guest feedback and communication trends

- Identify opportunities for operational improvement



Required Skills & Qualifications



Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Strong customer service mindset with confident problem-solving ability

Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively under pressure

High level of accuracy and attention to detail

Familiarity with short-term rental platforms (Airbnb, 
, Vrbo)

Proficiency in tools such as Breezeway, Hostaway, Outlook, and Teams

Ability to work independently and take full ownership of responsibilities

Reliable high-speed internet connection with backup



Work Schedule:

6 days a week

14 hours per day (availability across time zones may be required)

title: Sales/Admin VA

s and social media messages 
, Instagram, LinkedIn)

s per day

, social media platforms

title: Social Media Manager, Podcast Manager, Executive Assistant
description:
Full-Time Virtual Assistant — Media & Content Operations

Company: The AI Report

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Hours: Full-time, 40 hours/week (Monday–Friday)

Salary: $1,000–$1,200 USD/month (based on experience)

Start Date: Immediately



About Us

The AI Report is one of the fastest-growing AI media companies in the world — 400,000+ newsletter subscribers, 61% open rate, two active podcasts, a YouTube channel, and a Circle community for executives. We're based in Montreal, Canada.



We're hiring a full-time VA to own our day-to-day content operations so our CEO can focus on growth, partnerships, and speaking engagements. This is a high-trust, high-responsibility role — not data entry.



Important: This role requires near-native English fluency — both written and spoken. You'll be writing public-facing content (show notes, YouTube descriptions, community posts) and communicating directly with the CEO daily via voice and video. If English isn't your strongest language, this isn't the right fit.



What You'll Do

Podcast & Audio Production (10–12 hrs/week)



Upload episodes to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and YouTube



Generate and attach transcripts using AI tools



Write show notes and episode descriptions



Coordinate guest scheduling via Calendly



Create and schedule audiogram clips for social media



YouTube Management (6–8 hrs/week)



Upload videos with optimized titles, descriptions, tags, and thumbnails



Manage playlists and end screens



Monitor comments and flag anything needing a response



Track performance metrics weekly



Community Management (4–6 hrs/week)



Upload content to our Circle community



Monitor community engagement and respond to basic questions



Organize and tag community resources



Design Support (4–6 hrs/week)



Create social media graphics, thumbnails, and visual assets in Canva



Maintain brand consistency across all visual content



Resize and adapt creatives for different platforms



Admin & Operations (8–10 hrs/week)



Triage the CEO's inbox (Gmail)



Update contacts and deal stages in Notion CRM



Calendar management and meeting prep



Basic research tasks as needed



Maintain SOPs and checklists in Notion



Required Skills

Near-native English fluency — written and spoken. You'll write public-facing content and speak with the CEO daily. This is non-negotiable.



Experience with YouTube (uploading, metadata, thumbnails)



Experience with podcast hosting platforms (Spotify for Podcasters, Apple Podcasts Connect, RSS)



Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, or similar) in your daily workflow



Comfortable working in Notion



Design skills — able to create and edit graphics in Canva or similar tools



Tech-savvy and fast learner — you'll also use Circle, Calendly, and Google Workspace



Self-starter who works independently, follows SOPs, and flags problems early



Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Nice-to-Have

Familiarity with the AI industry



Video editing experience



Experience supporting a CEO or founder directly



What Success Looks Like

Week 1: You've learned all our platforms, completed onboarding, and handled your first episode uploads and YouTube posts independently.



Month 1: You own the full podcast-to-publish pipeline. Episodes go live on time, every time. YouTube is updated same-day. Circle is active and organized. The CEO's inbox is clean.



Month 3: You're anticipating needs before being asked. You've improved our SOPs. You're flagging performance insights. The CEO spends zero time on operations.



Compensation & Growth

$1,000–$1,200 USD/month based on experience



Paid Philippine holidays



Performance review and raise opportunity at 90 days



Path to Content Operations Manager as the company scales



You'll work directly with the CEO — high-visibility role with real impact



How to Apply

Record a short video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself, your experience, and why you're a good fit. Upload to YouTube (unlisted) or Loom and share the link.



Attach your resume or portfolio



Answer these questions in your application:



What is your experience with YouTube channel management? Give a specific example.



Have you worked with podcast hosting platforms before? Which ones?



Describe a time you managed multiple recurring tasks with tight deadlines. How did you stay organized?



What AI tools do you currently use, and how do you use them in your work?



What is your current internet speed? (Run a test at speedtest.net and share the result)

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Reels/TikTok) — High Volume, Performance-Based
description:
We’re looking for a fast, detail-oriented video editor to join our team.



This is not a cinematic editing role.

This is about creating high-performing short-form content (Instagram Reels / TikTok) at scale.



What You’ll Do



* Turn raw footage into multiple short-form videos

* Find the best 
nts (hooks) in longer clips

* Add captions, cuts, and simple effects

* Repurpose 1 video into multiple pieces of content

* Schedule posts using tools like Later or Metricool

* Organize files in Google Drive



What We’re Looking For



* Experience editing short-form content (Reels/TikTok)

* Understands hooks, pacing, and retention

* Can produce multiple videos from the same footage

* Fast turnaround and consistent output

* Good English communication

* Reliable and organized



Important

We care more about:



* Your ability to pick viral 
nts

* Your ability to create multiple outputs



Than:



* Advanced effects or cinematic editing



Test Task (Required)

If shortlisted, you will receive:



* 3 raw video clips (1 minute each)



Your task:



* Create 5 short-form videos total

* Use different hooks/angles where possible

* Add captions



This helps us understand how you think and edit.



To Apply

1. Links to your best short-form edits

2. A short answer:

"How do you d
 what makes a good hook?"

3. Your availability (hours per week)



We are building a long-term team, so if you perform well, this can turn into a stable, ongoing role with more responsibility.



Looking forward to working with you.

The scope requires high attention to detail, superior analytical skills, patience with a lot of manual processing while mastering systems used in purchasing activity. Great communication skills, FLUENT ENGLISH and follow-through to ensure transactions are done on time, accurately and in line with divisional standards.

title: Video Editor & YouTube Strategist (Daily Reaction Content)
description:
About the Brand:

We run 'The Maximus Breakdown,' a highly active daily media brand focused on news, politics, and culture. We have a massive, established footprint on
 and Instagram, and our primary goal right now is to aggressively scale and monetize our YouTube channel. We need a reliable, sharp Video Editor to take over the daily production.



The Role:

This is a consistent, daily position. You will take raw reaction footage and turn it into a high-retention viewing experience.



Daily Long-Form: Edit ONE 8-12 minute 'Reaction Style' video per day.

Daily Short-Form: Extract 4-7 viral-ready 'Shorts' or 'Reels' from that daily long-form video.



The Editing Style: We do not use heavy B-roll. We focus on fast-paced 'Reaction' editing. This includes:

Dynamic Framing: Using punch-ins and zooms to emphasize facial reactions and key talking points.

Audio Mastering: Balancing the host's microphone audio with the original video audio so it sounds flawless.

On-Screen Graphics: Adding bold text call-outs for names or facts, and dynamic captions for the Shorts.



YouTube Growth & Bonus Skills:

We are looking for a partner to help build this channel. If you have proven experience growing a YouTube channel, that is a massive plus. If you can also create high-clickability thumbnails and handle the backend SEO/uploading, you will be moved to the front of the line.



Compensation & Growth:

Starting Salary: $75 USD per week.

Workload: 1 long-form edit + 4-7 shorts daily (Approximately 1.5–2 hours of work per day).

Growth: This is a performance-based partnership. As you help grow the YouTube channel, increase views, and boost monetization, your pay will increase right alongside it. We want you to win as the brand wins.



How to Apply:

Please send a link to your portfolio showing your best fast-paced or reaction-style edits. If you have YouTube management or thumbnail experience, include examples. To prove you pay attention to details, start your application with the word: POWER.

To apply, send your portfolio and a brief intro about yourself.
